title: If You Use Agent Zero, This Video is for You (EPIC FAIL)
summary: Jordan Urbs documents a failed attempt to build an AI ad campaign generator using Agent Zero, encountering persistent LiteLLM errors that made the framework unusable. He concludes that Agent Zero shows promise but advises testing thoroughly before committing production workflows, noting Claude Code or OpenClaw would have completed the task.
writeup: A hands-on attempt to use Agent Zero to build a full AI-generated video ad campaign for an online course, wiring together a Creative Director skill and a Venice video model routing skill for character-consistent video generation. The session runs 5+ hours, produces 10 scored ad concepts through a structured ideation pipeline (intake, insight, ideation, evaluate, refine, articulate), but breaks down irreparably on persistent LLM context errors tied to the custom project settings. The through-line is a frank reassessment of autonomous agent frameworks: when a framework fails with errors you cannot diagnose or fix, the setup cost becomes a liability rather than an asset.
key_points: Agent Zero's Venice video model routing skill routes to different Venice models by shot type: character consistency uses Cling V3 multi-shot and Cling V3 Pro, atmosphere shots use V03.1, and a Nano Banana Pro two-pass step generates the reference images used as video input frames.

The Creative Director skill applies a six-phase framework (intake, insight, ideation, evaluate, refine, articulate) that surfaces tension mining, scores concepts numerically, and drops any concept below threshold before surfacing the final 10, all without manual curation.

The campaign math called for 5 base ads, each tested with 3 hooks and 3 CTAs, yielding up to 45 ad variants, targeted at Reels and Stories placements with a lifetime budget and a full-conversion objective.

Claude Sonnet 4.6 was used as the Agent Zero backbone instead of Opus to conserve Venice DM credits, where 1 DM equals 1 USD and refreshes daily, making Opus prohibitively fast to drain on a long agentic run.

After 5+ hours, a recurring LLM error appeared exclusively inside the custom marketing project context but not in a blank project, blocking all progress despite workarounds including attaching new markdown files and restarting conversations.

The presenter's conclusion is direct: Agent Zero's ambition to act immediately rather than entering a plan-review loop (unlike Claude Code's explicit plan mode) makes it harder to control, and a framework that can fail irrecoverably after hours of setup may not justify the investment over simpler alternatives like Claude Code with a custom API router.
